title: File Integrity Monitoring Alerts on Payment Web Roots

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 11.5.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 11.5.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.70: File Integrity Monitoring Alerts on Payment Web Roots.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.70' running on index=os sourcetype="ossec" or auditd paths; sourcetype="stash" from monitor:// with hash in change_management, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
spl:
index=os (sourcetype="linux:audit" OR sourcetype="ossec") earliest=-24h
| where match(name,"(?i)/var/www/payment|/opt/checkout/dist)")
| where match(key,"(?i)write|attrib|chmod|unlink|create)")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_change, values(key) as ops by host, name
| sort - first_change
description: Surfaces Splunk Universal Forwarder or OSSEC/AIDE change alerts on directories serving checkout static content so unauthorized modification of critical files is detected promptly.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes os and sourcetypes linux:audit, ossec, stash. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Scope FIM to PCI web roots only to reduce noise. (2) Correlate with deploy pipeline user. (3) Page on off-hours changes. (4) Store weekly digest for change-detection evidence (Req 11 overlap). Indexes required: `index=os`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=linux:audit`, `sourcetype=ossec`, `sourcetype=stash`.
visualization: Table (file paths), timeline (first_change), heatmap (host x hour).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux

knownFalsePositives: FIM alerts from authorized patches, application updates, or vendor-provided config changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=os` `sourcetype="ossec"` or `auditd` paths; `sourcetype="stash"` from `monitor://` with `hash` in `change_management`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Scope FIM to PCI web roots only to reduce noise. (2) Correlate with deploy pipeline user. (3) Page on off-hours changes. (4) Store weekly digest for change-detection evidence (Req 11 overlap).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=os (sourcetype="linux:audit" OR sourcetype="ossec") earliest=-24h
| where match(name,"(?i)/var/www/payment|/opt/checkout/dist)")
| where match(key,"(?i)write|attrib|chmod|unlink|create)")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_change, values(key) as ops by host, name
| sort - first_change
```

Understanding this SPL

**File Integrity Monitoring Alerts on Payment Web Roots (PCI DSS Req 10.5.2, 11.5.1)** — Surfaces Splunk Universal Forwarder or OSSEC/AIDE change alerts on directories serving checkout static content so unauthorized modification of critical files is detected promptly.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=os` `sourcetype="ossec"` or `auditd` paths; `sourcetype="stash"` from `monitor://` with `hash` in `change_management`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: os; **sourcetype**: linux:audit, ossec. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=os, sourcetype="linux:audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(name,"(?i)/var/www/payment|/opt/checkout/dist)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(key,"(?i)write|attrib|chmod|unlink|create)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**File Integrity Monitoring Alerts on Payment Web Roots (PCI DSS Req 10.5.2, 11.5.1)** — Surfaces Splunk Universal Forwarder or OSSEC/AIDE change alerts on directories serving checkout static content so unauthorized modification of critical files is detected promptly.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=os` `sourcetype="ossec"` or `auditd` paths; `sourcetype="stash"` from `monitor://` with `hash` in `change_management`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (file paths), timeline (first_change), heatmap (host x hour).
grandmaExplanation: We surface Splunk Universal Forwarder or OSSEC/AIDE change alerts on directories serving checkout static content so unauthorized modification of critical files is detected promptly. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Change (CIM data model), All_Changes dataset.
